title: Write shaders that are defined on every driver
description: Avoid undefined behavior and driver-specific assumptions in shader code so output is consistent across GPUs and APIs.
body: Use when authoring or porting GLSL/HLSL/WGSL. Do not read uninitialized variables, take derivatives (dFdx/textureLod bias) inside non-uniform control flow, or rely on a specific NaN/precision behavior. Actions: initialize all outputs, keep derivative and dynamic-texture operations in uniform control flow, declare precision explicitly where the API requires it, and compile against the validation/SPIR-V layer. Validation signals include a clean shader-compiler/validation pass and identical output across at least two drivers. Risk boundary: undefined shader behavior can render correctly on the dev GPU and corrupt elsewhere; treat any validation warning as a portability defect to fix before shipping.
